claim_summary: Free/Pro/Max/Teams pricing is public on official pricing and docs pages
note: Teams starts at $80/month; full seats and flex seat model described in docs

claim_summary: Cognition completed a $400M round at a reported $10.2B valuation
Cursor public pricing includes Pro $20/month and Teams $40/user/month
Cursor enterprise page claims 50,000+ businesses and 64% of Fortune 500 use Cursor
Windsurf public pricing includes Pro $20/month, Max $200/month and Teams $40/user/month
Windsurf homepage claims 1M+ users and 4,000+ enterprise customers
Augment public pricing starts at $20/month for Indie and $60/month for Standard
TechCrunch reported Augment launched with $252M raised at roughly $977M post-money valuation
Cline personal offering is free/open and enterprise offering emphasizes SSO, RBAC and observability
note: Enterprise packaging is explicit even if enterprise price is custom

claim_summary: Replit pricing includes Core $20/month and Teams $35/user/month with Enterprise custom
note: Agent access is part of paid plans

claim_summary: Replit officially announced a $250M funding round and claims millions of users and 500,000+ professional users
Dify pricing includes Professional $59/workspace/month and Enterprise sales motion
note: Self-host and enterprise paths are explicit

claim_summary: Dify announced a $30M Pre-A round and states over a million apps are running on Dify
Dify announced renewed SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001 and GDPR compliance
Lovable pricing includes Pro $25/month and Business $50/month with Enterprise custom
Lovable announced $330M Series B at a $6.6B valuation
Lovable announced ARR milestones above $100M and later $200M with 10M+ projects
note: Later anniversary post updates the numbers

claim_summary: Trae public pricing is free for individuals and ¥49/¥99/¥199 per user per month for enterprise tiers
Official Volcano Engine materials state 6M+ registered users by 2025-12 and 1M+ monthly active users
Trae enterprise page advertises knowledge base, custom agents, model access, audit logs and VPC/private network options
Lingma pricing shows free individual base tier, enterprise standard ¥79/user/month and enterprise dedicated ¥159/user/month
Official case pages claim service to over ten thousand enterprises and over one million developers
Alibaba help center says Lingma will be renamed Qoder CN on 2026-05-20
Comate public pricing shows free individual standard tier, ¥100/month individual professional, and enterprise tiers up to private deployment
Baidu states Wenkuaima has served 10M+ developers and 10,000+ enterprises
OpenHands should productize enterprise controls and ROI proof instead of competing on benchmark narrative alone
Continue public pricing is $3 per million tokens for Starter and $20 per seat per month for Team
Continue VS Code extension has over 3 million installs
Aider is Apache-2.0 open source and relies on user-provided model/API keys rather than a public first-party SaaS seat price
Aider homepage shows about 44K GitHub stars and 6.8M installs
note: Community adoption signal, not direct sales proof

claim_summary: Manus public plans include Free, Pro and Team, with Pro from $20/month and Team from $20 per seat per month
Manus officially states ARR above $100M and revenue run-rate above $125M
Bolt public pricing includes Pro $25/month and Teams $30 per user per month with Enterprise custom pricing
Bolt enterprise materials show Microsoft Marketplace distribution, enterprise security packaging and a named Digital Virgo case
note: Packaging is strong; broad financial transparency is weaker

claim_summary: CodeGeeX enterprise documentation clearly supports private deployment, enterprise management and knowledge base integration, but no public pricing page was found
MarsCode has effectively been folded into Trae plugin branding and no longer presents a clearly independent commercial SKU
note: Useful as historical entry strategy, weak as standalone product comparison
